title: A decoder-only foundation model for time-series forecasting (TimesFM)
authors: Abhimanyu Das, Weihao Kong, Rajat Sen, Yichen Zhou (Google Research)
tokenization_method: Patch tokenization of a univariate real-valued series, NOT value-quantization. The context y_1:L is scaled (reversible-instance-normalization-style: subtract mean, divide by std computed on the FIRST input patch only) then cut into contiguous, non-overlapping patches of length input_patch_len p=32. Each patch y_tilde_j = y_{p(j-1)+1:pj} is element-wise multiplied by (1 - mask) to zero out padded/missing points, then mapped to a model_dim vector by an InputResidualBlock (a 1-hidden-layer MLP with a skip connection); a learned positional encoding PE_j is added: t_j = InputResidualBlock(y_tilde_j ⊙ (1 - m_tilde_j)) + PE_j. So a token is a continuous learned embedding of a 32-point window — there is no codebook and no discretization. The OutputResidualBlock maps each output token o_j to a vector of length output_patch_len h=128, i.e. the forecast for the h points following the last input point covered by patch j. A binary padding/missing mask m_1:L rides alongside the values; a patch-token is marked masked only if EVERY point in the patch is masked (dot-m_j = min over the patch).
feature_types_handled: numerical only (univariate continuous time series). Explicitly NO dynamic covariates and NO static covariates: 'since we are building a single pre-trained model, we cannot have dataset specific dynamic or static covariates during training time.' No categorical inputs. Missing/short context is handled only via the padding mask (zero-pad to a multiple of p, mark padded points masked). No timestamp/calendar feature is fed; frequency/granularity is implicit in the data only.
token_granularity: temporal patch — one token = one contiguous window of input_patch_len=32 consecutive time-points, embedded by an MLP residual block to model_dim. Output token decodes a longer output_patch_len=128 window. Not per-value, not per-feature, not a learned discrete code.
numerical_strategy: Raw scalars within a patch are kept continuous (no binning, no piecewise-linear, no periodic encoding). Normalization = the scaling half of reversible instance norm (RevIN): scale the whole context by the mean and std of the FIRST input patch only (not the global series, not per-patch). The 32-vector patch is then projected by the input residual MLP. Distance/ordinality is preserved natively because the loss is MSE in the original (rescaled) value space, unlike Chronos's distance-unaware cross-entropy over bins.
high_cardinality_handling: n/a — no categorical or ID features at all.
temporal_handling: The entire model is temporal. Decoder-only (causal self-attention) over the patch sequence: each output token must predict the window after the last input point it has seen, trained in parallel over the whole context like an LLM. Key non-LLM twist: output_patch_len (128) > input_patch_len (32) so each autoregressive step emits a long chunk, cutting the number of AR steps at inference (e.g. horizon 256 = 2 steps not 8). Variable context handled by a random patch-masking scheme: per series, sample r in [0, p-1], mask the first r points of patch 1, so across r the model is trained on all context lengths 1..512 that are not just multiples of p. Inference: append zeros + mask to make L a multiple of p, then AR-decode h points at a time.
scales_to_many_features: Does NOT scale to many features — strictly single-channel univariate, by design (foundation model trained without any covariates). It scales in the orthogonal sense of model/data size (17M->70M->200M params, O(100B) timepoints, monotone Scaled-MAE improvement with FLOPS) and in sequence length (patching cuts token count by ~p=32x vs per-point, improving attention cost and inference speed). For a 70+ heterogeneous-feature setting it offers a per-channel encoder at best; cross-feature fusion is entirely out of scope.
downstream_model: A custom decoder-only stacked Transformer (num_layers transformer blocks, standard multi-head causal self-attention + FFN with hidden size = model_dim). Headline model = 200M parameters. Input/output are bespoke MLP residual blocks, not an off-the-shelf LM (contrast Chronos, which reuses T5/GPT-2 unchanged). Trained on TPUv5e (16 cores), ~2 days for 1.5M iterations at the 200M scale, global batch 4096.
pretraining_objective: Point-forecast next-patch prediction with MSE loss: each output token's h-length forecast is regressed against the true following h points, averaged over all N patch positions (decoder-only, parallel over the window). NOT a probabilistic/cross-entropy objective. The paper notes probabilistic forecasting is an easy extension via multiple quantile heads (pinball loss) or a parametric-distribution likelihood head, but the released pretraining uses MSE point loss.
key_results: Zero-shot, a single 200M TimesFM model comes close to fully-supervised, dataset-specific SOTA across three out-of-corpus benchmark families: (a) Monash (mean Scaled-MAE, geometric mean of MAE scaled by naive baseline) — TimesFM is the top model; (b) Darts (8 univariate datasets) — TimesFM within statistical significance of the best per-dataset-tuned baselines; (c) Informer/ETT long-horizon (horizons 96 and 192, context 512, 8 tasks) — TimesFM best, with supervised PatchTST within significance; llmtime beaten by PatchTST. Achieved at modest scale: 200M params, O(100B) timepoints. Scaling study shows monotone Scaled-MAE decrease vs FLOPS across 17M/70M/200M.
baselines_compared: Supervised/task-specific deep: PatchTST (long-horizon SOTA), N-BEATS, FEDformer, DeepAR, and the Monash/Darts statistical and supervised baselines (ARIMA/ETS/Theta, etc., scaled by naive). Zero-shot/LLM-based: llmtime. PatchTST is the closest architectural comparator (TimesFM = decoder-only patching vs PatchTST encoder-style patching).
transferable_idea: Patch-as-token for the time-varying half of a heterogeneous feature set: take each numeric/temporal channel's recent window, RevIN-scale it (mean/std of an anchor window), cut into fixed-length patches (p~16-32), and embed each patch with a small MLP residual block into a model_dim token — then a transformer attends over the patch sequence. This is a *continuous* tokenizer (MSE in value space, ordinality preserved) and is far cheaper than per-point tokens (token count cut by p). The longer-output-patch trick (h>p) is a reusable decoding-efficiency idea when horizons are long and unknown. The mechanism is per-channel; fusing the resulting time-varying patch-tokens with static/categorical tokens into one sequence is the bridge the topic must build itself.
limitations: (1) Univariate only — no covariate, multivariate, or static-attribute mechanism, deferred entirely. (2) Point-forecast (MSE) by default; uncertainty needs the un-released quantile/distribution heads. (3) Pretraining corpus skews to well-represented granularities (hourly/daily from Google Trends + Wiki Pageviews); underrepresented frequencies (quarterly, yearly, 10-min) generalize worse — the synthetic-data ablation exists specifically to patch this and removing synthetic data drops Monash (esp. ETTm 15-min) accuracy. (4) output_patch_len too long breaks on series shorter than it; too short loses the one-shot-decoding benefit. (5) input_patch_len too large drifts toward encoder-decoder training and loses the decoder-only any-context property. (6) No exogenous/event handling; calendar features ignored.
pitfalls: Easy to conflate with Chronos: TimesFM does NOT quantize values into a vocabulary — its tokens are continuous MLP embeddings of patches and its loss is MSE, the opposite of Chronos's bin-vocabulary + cross-entropy. The scaling is RevIN computed on the FIRST input patch only, not the whole context and not per-patch — misremembering this changes the normalization. 'Decoder-only' here means causal next-PATCH prediction trained in parallel, plus the deliberate h>p asymmetry, which is NOT how LLMs decode (one token at a time) — the asymmetry is a core design choice, not an afterthought. The random-mask-the-first-r-points-of-patch-1 trick is the entire reason arbitrary (non-multiple-of-p) context lengths work; without it the model only handles context lengths that are multiples of 32. Zero-shot wins are genuinely out-of-corpus (Monash/Darts/Informer not in training) but Electricity/Traffic/Weather/M4 ARE in the training mix, so those are not zero-shot — the headline zero-shot claim applies to the held-out benchmark families only.
relevance_to_topic: Anchor for the timeseries-foundation-models route: a clean, continuous patch tokenizer + decoder-only forecaster that is the leading alternative tokenization to value-binning (Chronos) and to inverted/channel tokens (iTransformer) for the time-varying half of a 70+-feature set — but it provides zero machinery for categorical/static/multi-channel fusion, which is the topic's hard part.
code_available: yes — https://github.com/google-research/timesfm (Apache-2.0, maintained; pretrained checkpoints TimesFM-1.0-200M and later 2.x on HuggingFace google/timesfm-*).
